title: castilleja ambigua var. insalutata
intro: Castilleja ambigua var. insalutata, also known as Indian paintbrush, is a perennial plant species belonging to the figwort family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, and as a cough suppressant. The plant's leaves and flowers are used in herbal preparations, which are said to offer health benefits including reducing inflammation and acting as an expectorant. Preparations of Castilleja ambigua var. insalutata can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
